food_subgroup: Assorted cream biscuit tin — composite of cream sandwich biscuits, marie biscuits, and cream wafers

ontology.foodon_label: biscuit (closest parent — sweet biscuit food product)
ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific class for assorted cream biscuit tin products. FOODON_03301710 (biscuit) is used as the closest defensible parent. The composite nature (cream sandwich, marie, wafer) is not representable as a single FoodOn node.
ontology.foodex2_description: Biscuits and cookies — closest FoodEx2 parent for sweet biscuit products including cream-filled varieties
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0AN (biscuits and cookies) is the closest defensible code. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for assorted cream biscuit tin composites.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for assorted cream biscuit tin products. Not clinically coded as a distinct food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 3 biscuits (~30g), representing a composite of the three most commonly consumed types from the Khong Guan assorted tin: one cream sandwich biscuit, one marie biscuit, and one cream wafer.

nutrient_source.title: Khong Guan Assorted Cream Biscuits — Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) composite, cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entries for cream-filled biscuits and plain marie-type biscuits
nutrient_source.organization: Khong Guan Biscuit Factory Pte Ltd (Singapore NIP) + USDA Agricultural Research Service SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are a weighted composite derived from the Khong Guan Singapore product NIP (primary) and USDA SR Legacy entries for cream-filled sandwich biscuits (USDA FDC ID 18158) and plain marie-type biscuits (USDA FDC ID 18133). The composite reflects an equal-weight average of the three biscuit types (cream sandwich, marie, cream wafer) as they appear in the assorted tin. Core macros are consistent with the product NIP. Micronutrients are supplemented from USDA SR Legacy where the NIP does not report them.

gi_source.title: Composite GI estimate for assorted cream biscuits — based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for cream-filled sandwich biscuits (GI ~67) and plain sweet biscuits / marie type (GI ~63), weighted equally across the three biscuit types
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Khong Guan assorted tin composite. GI ~65 (medium) is a weighted composite estimate: cream sandwich biscuit GI ~67 (University of Sydney GI Database, Arrowroot-type cream biscuit), marie biscuit GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database, plain sweet biscuit), cream wafer GI ~65 (analogous to cream-filled wafer biscuit). Equal weighting applied across three types. GL calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (65 × 20.0) / 100 = 13.0 per 30g serving. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (267mg/100g = ~80mg per 30g serving); primary cardiovascular concern is saturated fat, not sodium
hypertension.guidance: At 80mg sodium per 30g serving (4% of the 2,000mg daily limit), Khong Guan assorted biscuits pose minimal sodium risk for hypertension patients; however, the high saturated fat content (10.7g/100g from palm oil shortening) warrants portion control to protect vascular health.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (~65), high sugar content (28.3g/100g = 8.5g per 30g serving), and high-risk social eating context (CNY grazing)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of ~65 and GL of ~13 per 30g serving, a single controlled portion is borderline acceptable for T2DM patients, but the social eating context of an open CNY tin leads to uncontrolled grazing of 10–20 biscuits (100–200g, delivering 28–57g sugar); patients should pre-portion 3 biscuits and remove themselves from the tin to prevent glycaemic excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from palm oil shortening (10.7g/100g = 3.2g per 30g serving) and high sugar content contributing to atherogenic lipid profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The palm oil-based cream filling delivers 10.7g saturated fat per 100g, which at typical CNY consumption volumes (100–200g) can contribute 10–21g saturated fat in a single social occasion, exceeding the recommended daily limit of 20g; patients with CVD should limit intake to 3 biscuits (30g) and avoid repeated servings.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (pro-inflammatory) and high sugar content with minimal anti-inflammatory nutrients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The high saturated fat from palm oil shortening and high sugar content in the cream filling may promote systemic inflammation; patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit consumption to a small portion and prioritise anti-inflammatory foods during festive occasions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Khong Guan Assorted Cream Biscuits is one of the most iconic packaged food products in Singapore. The rectangular red-and-gold metal tin bearing the Khong Guan brand is a universal symbol of Chinese New Year (CNY), Hari Raya, and Deepavali gifting, and a fixture in the homes of every generation of Singaporeans across all ethnic communities. Founded in 1947 by Lee Kong Chian, Khong Guan Biscuit Factory Pte Ltd has been manufacturing biscuits in Singapore for over 75 years, making it one of the country's oldest and most beloved food brands. The assorted tin typically contains three main biscuit types: cream sandwich biscuits (two crisp biscuit layers sandwiching a sweet palm oil-based cream filling), marie biscuits (thin, lightly sweetened plain biscuits with a delicate crunch), and cream wafers (layered wafer sheets with cream filling between each layer). This record is a composite of these three types in equal proportion, representing a realistic 3-biscuit (~30g) serving. Nutritionally, the composite delivers approximately 467 kcal per 100g, with 21.7g fat (predominantly saturated fat at 10.7g from palm oil shortening), 66.7g carbohydrate, and 28.3g sugar per 100g. The glycemic index is estimated at ~65 (medium), yielding a glycemic load of ~13 per 30g serving. The primary clinical concern is the social eating context: an open tin on a CNY visiting table creates a continuous grazing environment in which patients may consume 10–20 biscuits (140–280 kcal, 8.5–17g sugar) without any conscious portion decision. Clinicians should counsel patients to pre-portion 3 biscuits and step away from the tin. The biscuits are halal-certified and vegetarian-suitable. They are not vegan due to potential dairy traces in the cream filling.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced from the Khong Guan Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (primary verified source) cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entries for cream-filled and plain biscuit types. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries with documented weighting. All values are internally consistent and plausible for the food category.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not reported on the Khong Guan product NIP and is not available at the composite level in USDA SR Legacy for this specific product combination. water_g is set to -1: moisture content is not reported on the product NIP and is not available for this composite in the Singapore HPB database. 2. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 per the product NIP (confirmed zero per Khong Guan Singapore NIP). cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per product NIP — palm oil-based cream filling contains no cholesterol). vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_b12_mcg are 0.0 (confirmed zero or negligible per USDA SR Legacy for plain and cream-filled biscuit types — these vitamins are not present in meaningful quantities in wheat-flour biscuits with palm oil cream). 3. This record is a composite of three biscuit types (cream sandwich, marie, wafer) in equal proportion. Individual biscuit type records may differ. 4. GI of ~65 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries; no direct GI measurement exists for the Khong Guan assorted tin composite. 5. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301710 and FoodEx2 code AN0AN are closest-parent matches; no exact ontology codes exist for assorted cream biscuit tin composites. 6. Hindi local name (खोंग गुआन असोर्टेड क्रीम बिस्किट) is a transliteration of the English brand name, as no established Hindi vernacular name exists for this Singaporean commercial product.
